channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: This World Cup marks end of era for Ronaldo and other soccer greats
item.description: Some of the greatest footballers of a generation played their final World Cup match this summer.
item.content:encoded:
Some of the greatest footballers of a generation played their final World Cup match this summer.

(Image credit: Lars Baron)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Supreme Court lets Texas restrict minors' access to app stores for the time being
item.description: Texas' App Store Accountability Act requires minors to have their parents' permission to download most apps. The Supreme Court says the law can go into effect as lawsuits continue in lower courts.
item.content:encoded:
Texas' App Store Accountability Act requires minors to have their parents' permission to download most apps. The Supreme Court says the law can go into effect as lawsuits continue in lower courts.

(Image credit: Paul Ellis)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Platner denies sexual assault allegation, but says he will assess 'best path forward'
item.description: The Democratic candidate for Senate in Maine said he was assessing next steps after allegations of sexual assault were reported on Monday by Politico. Platner denied them as "categorically untrue."
item.content:encoded:
The Democratic candidate for Senate in Maine said he was assessing next steps after allegations of sexual assault were reported on Monday by Politico. Platner denied them as "categorically untrue."

(Image credit: Laura Brett)
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore

item.title: Man sues DHS after agents tracked him down for sending a scathing email to ICE
item.description: Federal agents tried to track David Streever to his home and hotel, and left him a warning notice that a critical email he sent the former head of ICE may have been illegal. Now he's suing.
item.content:encoded:
Federal agents tried to track David Streever to his home and hotel, and left him a warning notice that a critical email he sent the former head of ICE may have been illegal. Now he's suing.

(Image credit: David Streever)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: Militant LGBTQ+ rights group 'the Lavender Panthers' was founded on this day in 1973
item.description: "Reverend Ray" Broshears founded the queer vigilante group the Lavender Panthers in 1973. The group's impact is still felt today.
item.content:encoded: "Reverend Ray" Broshears founded the queer vigilante group the Lavender Panthers in 1973. The group's impact is still felt today.
item.dc:creator: Abi Inman

item.title: Historic World Cup furor at 'incomprehensible' FIFA decision to let U.S. forward Balogun play
item.description: The Belgian soccer federation is challenging FIFA's ruling to let Balogun play despite getting a red card in his previous game. Belgium's statement came just 11 hours before kickoff in Seattle. European soccer body UEFA criticizes FIFA's "incomprehensible and unjustifiable decision."
item.content:encoded:
The Belgian soccer federation is challenging FIFA's ruling to let Balogun play despite getting a red card in his previous game. Belgium's statement came just 11 hours before kickoff in Seattle. European soccer body UEFA criticizes FIFA's "incomprehensible and unjustifiable decision."

(Image credit: Chris Carlson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump, Mamdani give contrasting July 4th speeches. And, U.S. faces Belgium in World Cup
item.description: For the nation's 250th birthday, President Trump and NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani had opposing visions of America. And, the U.S. and Belgium face off today in the FIFA World Cup.
item.content:encoded:
For the nation's 250th birthday, President Trump and NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani had opposing visions of America. And, the U.S. and Belgium face off today in the FIFA World Cup.

(Image credit: Alex Wroblewski)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Hate food waste? 7 creative ways to turn your leftovers into a new meal
item.description: We asked our audience to share their favorite go-to recipes for leftovers. Here are seven dishes — like stuffed peppers and a biryani casserole — that can help you use up all your fridge scraps.
item.content:encoded:
We asked our audience to share their favorite go-to recipes for leftovers. Here are seven dishes — like stuffed peppers and a biryani casserole — that can help you use up all your fridge scraps.

(Image credit: kaisersosa67/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Malaka Gharib

item.title: These Medicare beneficiaries thought their drug plan was free. Then they lost it
item.description: Thousands of people lost coverage over as little as $8 in delinquent payments. They didn't know their zero-dollar premiums had gone up and they owed money. Most now can't get coverage until 2027.
item.content:encoded:
Thousands of people lost coverage over as little as $8 in delinquent payments. They didn't know their zero-dollar premiums had gone up and they owed money. Most now can't get coverage until 2027.  

(Image credit: fstop123/iStockphoto)
item.dc:creator: Susan Jaffe

item.title: Fast walkers in their 80s cut their risk of cognitive decline by half, a study finds
item.description: A new study of people 80 and older with exceptional gaits finds fast walkers have about a 50% lower risk of cognitive decline, showing the connection between physical health and brain health.
item.content:encoded:
A new study of people 80 and older with exceptional gaits finds fast walkers have about a 50% lower risk of cognitive decline, showing the connection between physical health and brain health.

(Image credit: FG Trade)
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey
